key_insight: A pretrained foundation-model MLIP can be adaptively fine-tuned inside an evolutionary CSP loop to cut DFT labeling cost while still finding stable crystal phases.
engineering_relevance: This workflow makes crystal structure prediction more scalable for complex multicomponent materials, enabling faster phase-diagram discovery under extreme conditions.
method: Start with a pretrained machine-learning interatomic potential for rapid structure exploration.
Use evolutionary CSP to generate candidate crystals and iteratively select compact, representative subsets for DFT labeling.
Fine-tune the model self-consistently as new labels arrive to improve accuracy for the target chemical system.
evidence_signal: In the Ca–Fe–Ni system, the method reproduced the known low-pressure convex hull and predicted a new compound, Ca6FeNi, stable above 100 GPa.
watch_item: Performance still depends on the quality of candidate selection and the pretrained model’s transferability to the new chemical space; poor coverage could miss relevant phases.
paper_title: Adaptive fine-tuning of foundation models for crystal structure prediction: Discovery of high-pressure phases in the CaFeNi system
